heading: Definition of taxable transportation
label: 4262 Definition of taxable transportation
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 4262 was renumbered 4263 of this title and later repealed.

children.text: transportation by air which begins in the United States or in the 225–mile zone and ends in the United States or in the 225–mile zone; and

in the case of transportation by air other than transportation described in paragraph (1), that portion of such transportation which is directly or indirectly from one port or station in the United States to another port or station in the United States, but only if such portion is not a part of uninterrupted international air transportation (within the meaning of subsection (c)(3)).
such portion is outside the United States;

children.label: A
children.text: between (i) a point where the route of the transportation leaves or enters the continental United States, or (ii) a port or station in the 225-mile zone, and

children.label: B
children.text: a port or station in the 225-mile zone;

children.label: A
children.text: begins at either (i) the point where the route of the transportation leaves the United States, or (ii) a port or station in the 225-mile zone, and

children.label: B
children.text: ends at either (i) the point where the route of the transportation enters the United States, or (ii) a port or station in the 225-mile zone; and

a direct line from the point (or the port or station) specified in paragraph (3)(A), to the point (or the port or station) specified in paragraph (3)(B), passes through or over a point which is not within 225 miles of the United States.

children.heading: Continental United States
children.label: 1 Continental United States
children.text: The term “continental United States” means the District of Columbia and the States other than Alaska and Hawaii.

children.heading: 225-mile zone
children.label: 2 225-mile zone
children.text: The term “225-mile zone” means that portion of Canada and Mexico which is not more than 225 miles from the nearest point in the continental United States.

children.label: A
children.text: the scheduled interval between (i) the beginning or end of the portion of such transportation which is directly or indirectly from one port or station in the United States to another port or station in the United States and (ii) the end or beginning of the other portion of such transportation is not more than 12 hours, and

children.label: B
children.text: the scheduled interval between the beginning or end and the end or beginning of any two segments of the portion of such transportation referred to in subparagraph (A)(i) is not more than 12 hours.

children.heading: Transportation
children.label: d Transportation
children.text: For purposes of this part, the term “transportation” includes layover or waiting time and movement of the aircraft in deadhead service.

children.heading: Authority to waive 225-mile zone provisions
children.label: e Authority to waive 225-mile zone provisions
A
children.text: the Secretary shall publish a notice of such determination in the Federal Register, and

children.label: B
children.text: effective with respect to transportation beginning after the date specified in such notice, to the extent provided in the agreement, the term “225-mile zone” shall not include part or all of the country with respect to which such determination is made.

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary shall publish a notice of such determination in the Federal Register, and

children.label: B
children.text: subparagraph (B) of paragraph (1) shall cease to apply with respect to transportation beginning after the date specified in such notice.

children.label: A
children.text: setting forth that portion of such country which is not to be treated as within the 225-mile zone, and

children.label: B
children.text: providing that the tax imposed by such country on transportation described in subparagraph (A) will be at a level which the Secretary of the Treasury determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Requirement that agreement be submitted to Congress
children.label: 4 Requirement that agreement be submitted to Congress
children.text: No notice may be published under paragraph (1)(A) with respect to any qualified agreement before the date 90 days after the date on which a copy of such agreement was furnished to the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Finance of the Senate.
